Scenario: Authenticated employee submits complete indent with all required fields
Input / Action: POST /api/create_proposal/ with item_name, quantity, estimated_cost, designation, purpose
Expected Result: HTTP 200/201; IndentFile record created in DB; file_info linked

Scenario: Create indent as a draft instead of proposal
Unauthenticated user attempts to submit indent
PS admin creates GRN for fully received and accepted goods
Preconditions: StockEntry and IndentFile exist in DB
Input / Action: POST /api/grn/create/ with grn_number, quantity_received=2, quantity_accepted=2, quality_check_passed=True

Scenario: GRN created with delivery discrepancy (1 of 2 damaged)
Preconditions: StockEntry exists; goods received with damage
Input / Action: POST /api/grn/create/ with quantity_accepted=1, has_discrepancy=True, discrepancy_details filled

Scenario: Create GRN without mandatory grn_number field
User retrieves list of their own indents
Preconditions: User authenticated; indents exist for user
Expected Result: HTTP 200; list of IndentFile records returned

Scenario: User views indents inward to their designation
Preconditions: User holds a designation; indents forwarded to them

Scenario: Request indents for a non-existent username
Employee cancels their own ACTIVE indent with a valid reason
Preconditions: User is owner of indent; indent.status = ACTIVE
Expected Result: HTTP 200/204; indent.status = CANCELLED; record preserved (not deleted); audit log entry created

Scenario: HOD cancels an indent on behalf of department
Preconditions: User holds HOD designation; indent in ACTIVE state
Input / Action: POST /api/indents/{id}/cancel/ with {reason: 'Budget cut'} using HOD token

Scenario: Cancel a non-existent indent (invalid ID)
Expected Result: HTTP 404; 'Indent not found' error — NOT a 500

Scenario: Dept admin views current stock for their department
Expected Result: HTTP 200; stock item list for the department

Scenario: View stock items for specific item type
Student role attempts to access stock view
Check for unique item — no duplicates in system
Preconditions: No previous indent with same item_name and item_type

Scenario: Check item that already has similar indents
Expected Result: HTTP 200; duplicates list with existing matching indents

Scenario: Submit duplicate check without item_name field
Expected Result: HTTP 400; validation error: item_name is required

Scenario: PS admin rejects indent with a valid non-empty reason
Preconditions: Indent status = ACTIVE; user is PS admin

Scenario: HOD rejects indent forwarded to them
Input / Action: POST /api/indents/{id}/reject/ using HOD token with reason
Expected Result: HTTP 200/204; indent rejected; notification sent to creator

Scenario: Reject without providing any reason
Auditor lists all audit log entries
Preconditions: User holds Auditor designation; audit entries exist
Expected Result: HTTP 200; list of AuditLog records with action, user, timestamp, entity details

Scenario: Auditor filters audit logs by action type
Expected Result: HTTP 200; filtered log entries for CANCEL_INDENT actions only

Scenario: Student role attempts to view audit logs
Expected Result: HTTP 403; access denied — Auditor/Director role required

Scenario: HOD approves department indent
Preconditions: Indent exists; user holds HOD designation
Expected Result: HTTP 200/201; approval recorded; indent forwarded to next stage

Scenario: HOD rejects indent with comments
Preconditions: HOD authenticated; indent in ACTIVE state
Input / Action: POST /api/approve-indent/ with {file_id: N, approved: false, comments: 'Duplicate request'}

Scenario: Approve without providing file_id
PS admin creates a new vendor with unique code and valid GST/PAN
Input / Action: POST /api/vendors/create/ with vendor_code, vendor_name, gst_number, pan_number
Expected Result: HTTP 200/201; Vendor record created; is_approved=False by default

Scenario: List all registered vendors
Preconditions: User authenticated; at least one vendor exists
Expected Result: HTTP 200; list of all Vendor records with full details

Scenario: Unauthenticated request to vendor list
List all existing product return records
Create product return for identified discrepancy

Expected Result: HTTP 200; list of Tender records with status and bid counts
